description: Microsoft implements this Security Assessment and Authorization control

name: 021f8078-41a0-40e6-81b6-c6597da9f3ee
display_name: Kubernetes cluster container images should not include latest image tag
description: Requires that container images do not use the latest tag in Kubernetes, it is a best practice to ensure reproducibility, prevent unintended updates, and facilitate easier debugging and rollbacks by using explicit and versioned container images.

name: 03a4ecdb-0684-4039-be91-2762979e1bc8
display_name: Kubernetes cluster containers CPU and memory resource requests must be defined
description: Enforce container CPU and memory resource requests to ensure scheduled node has required resources.

name: 077f0ce1-86d6-4058-bc60-de05067e8622
display_name: Kubernetes cluster Windows pods should not run HostProcess containers
description: Prevent prviledged access to the windows node. This recommendation is intended to improve the security of Windows nodes. For more information, see https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/windows/intro/ .

name: 098fc59e-46c7-4d99-9b16-64990e543d75
display_name: Kubernetes cluster pod hostPath volumes should only use allowed host paths
description: Limit pod HostPath volume mounts to the allowed host paths in a Kubernetes Cluster. This policy is generally available for Kubernetes Service (AKS), and Azure Arc enabled Kubernetes. For more information, see https://aka.ms/kubepolicydoc.

name: 0a075868-4c26-42ef-914c-5bc007359560
display_name: Certificates should have the specified maximum validity period
description: Manage your organizational compliance requirements by specifying the maximum amount of time that a certificate can be valid within your key vault.

name: 0f636243-1b1c-4d50-880f-310f6199f2cb
display_name: [Deprecated]: Ensure containers listen only on allowed ports in AKS
description: This policy enforces containers to listen only on allowed ports in an Azure Kubernetes Service cluster. This policy is deprecated, please visit https://aka.ms/kubepolicydoc for instructions on using new Kubernetes policies.

name: 1151cede-290b-4ba0-8b38-0ad145ac888f
display_name: Certificates should use allowed key types
description: Manage your organizational compliance requirements by restricting the key types allowed for certificates.

name: 12db3749-7e03-4b9f-b443-d37d3fb9f8d9
display_name: Kubernetes cluster containers should only pull images when image pull secrets are present
description: Restrict containers' image pulls to enforce the presence of ImagePullSecrets, ensuring secure and authorized access to images within a Kubernetes cluster

name: 12e5dd16-d201-47ff-849b-8454061c293d
display_name: [Preview]: Azure Machine Learning Deployments should only use approved Registry Models
description: Restrict the deployment of Registry models to control externally created models used within your organization

name: 12ef42cb-9903-4e39-9c26-422d29570417
display_name: Certificates should have the specified lifetime action triggers
description: Manage your organizational compliance requirements by specifying whether a certificate lifetime action is triggered at a specific percentage of its lifetime or at a certain number of days prior to its expiration.

name: 13cd7ae3-5bc0-4ac4-a62d-4f7c120b9759
display_name: [Deprecated]: Kubernetes clusters should gate deployment of vulnerable images
description: This policy definition is no longer the recommended way to achieve its intent. Learn more about policy definition deprecation at aka.ms/policydefdeprecation.

name: 152b15f7-8e1f-4c1f-ab71-8c010ba5dbc0
display_name: Key Vault keys should have an expiration date
description: Cryptographic keys should have a defined expiration date and not be permanent. Keys that are valid forever provide a potential attacker with more time to compromise the key. It is a recommended security practice to set expiration dates on cryptographic keys.

name: 16697877-1118-4fb1-9b65-9898ec2509ec
display_name: Kubernetes cluster pods should only use allowed volume types
description: Pods can only use allowed volume types in a Kubernetes cluster. This policy is generally available for Kubernetes Service (AKS), and preview for Azure Arc enabled Kubernetes. For more information, see https://aka.ms/kubepolicydoc.

name: 16c6ca72-89d2-4798-b87e-496f9de7fcb7
display_name: [Deprecated]: Enforce labels on pods in AKS
description: This policy enforces the specified labels are provided for pods in an Azure Kubernetes Service cluster. This policy is deprecated, please visit https://aka.ms/kubepolicydoc for instructions on using new Kubernetes policies.

name: 19539b54-c61e-4196-9a38-67598701be90
display_name: [Preview]: Azure Machine Learning Model Registry Deployments are restricted except for the allowed Registry
description: Only deploy Registry Models in the allowed Registry and that are not restricted.

name: 1a3b9003-eac6-4d39-a184-4a567ace7645
display_name: Kubernetes cluster container images must include the preStop hook
description: Requires that container images include a preStop hook to gracefully terminate processes during pod shutdowns.

name: 1a5b4dca-0b6f-4cf5-907c-56316bc1bf3d
display_name: Kubernetes clusters should be accessible only over HTTPS
description: Use of HTTPS ensures authentication and protects data in transit from network layer eavesdropping attacks. This capability is currently generally available for Kubernetes Service (AKS), and in preview for Azure Arc enabled Kubernetes. For more info, visit https://aka.ms/kubepolicydoc

name: 1c6e92c9-99f0-4e55-9cf2-0c234dc48f99
display_name: Kubernetes clusters should not allow container privilege escalation
description: Do not allow containers to run with privilege escalation to root in a Kubernetes cluster. This recommendation is part of CIS 5.2.5 which is intended to improve the security of your Kubernetes environments. This policy is generally available for Kubernetes Service (AKS), and preview for Azure Arc enabled Kubernetes. For more information, see https://aka.ms/kubepolicydoc.

name: 1d413020-63de-11ea-bc55-0242ac130003
display_name: [Preview]: Configure log filter expressions and datastore to be used for full logs for specified Azure Machine Learning computes
description: Provide log filter expression and datastore to be used for full logs in specified Azure Machine Learning computes and can be assigned at the workspace. For more information, visit https://aka.ms/amlpolicydoc.

name: 1d478a74-21ba-4b9f-9d8f-8e6fced0eec5
display_name: [Preview]: Azure Key Vault Managed HSM keys should have an expiration date
description: To use this policy in preview, you must first follow these instructions at https://aka.ms/mhsmgovernance. Cryptographic keys should have a defined expiration date and not be permanent. Keys that are valid forever provide a potential attacker with more time to compromise the key. It is a recommended security practice to set expiration dates on cryptographic keys.

name: 1ddac26b-ed48-4c30-8cc5-3a68c79b8001
display_name: Kubernetes clusters should not allow endpoint edit permissions of ClusterRole/system:aggregate-to-edit
description: ClusterRole/system:aggregate-to-edit should not allow endpoint edit permissions due to CVE-2021-25740, Endpoint & EndpointSlice permissions allow cross-Namespace forwarding, https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/issues/103675. This policy is generally available for Kubernetes Service (AKS), and preview for Azure Arc enabled Kubernetes. For more information, see https://aka.ms/kubepolicydoc.

name: 1fd3b2cc-9904-40fa-b8da-050c02612da9
display_name: Azure Key Vault Managed HSM keys should be the specified cryptographic type
description: Some applications require the use of keys backed by a specific cryptographic type. Enforce a particular cryptographic key type, in your environment.

name: 233a2a17-77ca-4fb1-9b6b-69223d272a44
display_name: Kubernetes cluster services should listen only on allowed ports
description: Restrict services to listen only on allowed ports to secure access to the Kubernetes cluster. This policy is generally available for Kubernetes Service (AKS), and preview for Azure Arc enabled Kubernetes. For more information, see https://aka.ms/kubepolicydoc.

name: 25dee3db-6ce0-4c02-ab5d-245887b24077
display_name: [Deprecated]: Ensure services listen only on allowed ports in AKS
description: This policy enforces services to listen only on allowed ports in an Azure Kubernetes Service cluster. This policy is deprecated, please visit https://aka.ms/kubepolicydoc for instructions on using new Kubernetes policies.

name: 2ae2f266-ecc3-4d26-82c5-8c3cb7774f45
display_name: Sets readOnlyRootFileSystem in the Pod spec in init containers to true if it is not set.
description: Setting readOnlyRootFileSystem to true increases security by preventing containers from writing into the root filesystem. This works only for linux containers.

name: 2fbff515-eecc-4b7e-9b63-fcc7138b7dc3
display_name: [Deprecated]: Enforce HTTPS ingress in AKS
description: This policy enforces HTTPS ingress in an Azure Kubernetes Service cluster. This policy is deprecated, please visit https://aka.ms/kubepolicydoc for instructions on using new Kubernetes policies.

name: 2fe7ba7d-f670-41f5-8b70-b61dc7dfbe18
display_name: [Preview]: Prevents containers from being ran as root by setting runAsNotRoot to true.
description: Setting runAsNotRoot to true increases security by preventing containers from being ran as root.

name: 342e8053-e12e-4c44-be01-c3c2f318400f
display_name: Secrets should have the specified maximum validity period
description: Manage your organizational compliance requirements by specifying the maximum amount of time in days that a secret can be valid within your key vault.

name: 34c88cd4-5d72-4dbb-bf77-12c3cafe8791
display_name: Must Have Anti Affinity Rules or Topology Spread Constraints Set
description: This policy ensures that pods are scheduled on different nodes within the cluster. By enforcing anti-affinity rules or pod topology spread constraints, availability is maintained even if one of the nodes becomes unavailable. Pods will continue to run on other nodes, enhancing resilience.

name: 3948394e-63de-11ea-bc55-0242ac130003
display_name: [Preview]: Configure an approval endpoint called prior to jobs running for specified Azure Machine Learning computes
description: Configure an approval endpoint called prior to jobs running for specified Azure Machine Learning computes and can be assigned at the workspace. For more information. For more information, visit https://aka.ms/amlpolicydoc.

name: 3d02a511-74e5-4dab-a5fd-878704d4a61a
display_name: [Preview]: Azure Data Factory pipelines should only communicate with allowed domains
description: To prevent data & token exfiltration, set the domains that Azure Data Factory should be allowed to communicate with. Note: While in public preview, the compliance for this policy is not reported, & for policy to be applied to Data Factory, please enable outbound rules functionality in the ADF studio. For more information, visit https://aka.ms/data-exfiltration-policy.

name: 3fc4dc25-5baf-40d8-9b05-7fe74c1bc64e
display_name: Kubernetes clusters should use internal load balancers
description: Use internal load balancers to make a Kubernetes service accessible only to applications running in the same virtual network as the Kubernetes cluster. For more information, see https://aka.ms/kubepolicydoc.

name: 423dd1ba-798e-40e4-9c4d-b6902674b423
display_name: Kubernetes clusters should disable automounting API credentials
description: Disable automounting API credentials to prevent a potentially compromised Pod resource to run API commands against Kubernetes clusters. For more information, see https://aka.ms/kubepolicydoc.

name: 42ba1d72-e90f-42f8-bf99-5a1351eed2b1
display_name: Sets Kubernetes cluster containers CPU limits to default values in case not present.
description: Setting container CPU limits to prevent resource exhaustion attacks in a Kubernetes cluster.

name: 440b515e-a580-421e-abeb-b159a61ddcbc
display_name: [Deprecated]: Kubernetes cluster containers should only listen on allowed ports
description: Restrict containers to listen only on allowed ports to secure access to the Kubernetes cluster. The policy is deprecating since container port is only informative field which cannot decide the port container is actually using. For more information, see https://aka.ms/kubepolicydoc.

name: 46592696-4c7b-4bf3-9e45-6c2763bdc0a6
display_name: Kubernetes cluster pods should use specified labels
description: Use specified labels to identify the pods in a Kubernetes cluster. This policy is generally available for Kubernetes Service (AKS), and preview for Azure Arc enabled Kubernetes. For more information, see https://aka.ms/kubepolicydoc.

name: 47a1ee2f-2a2a-4576-bf2a-e0e36709c2b8
display_name: Kubernetes cluster containers should not share host namespaces
description: Block pod containers from sharing the host process ID namespace, host IPC namespace, and host network namespace in a Kubernetes cluster. This recommendation aligns with the Kubernetes Pod Security Standards for host namespaces and is part of CIS 5.2.1, 5.2.2 and 5.2.3 which are intended to improve the security of your Kubernetes environments. This policy is generally available for Kubernetes Service (AKS), and preview for Azure Arc enabled Kubernetes. For more information, see https://aka.ms/kubepolicydoc.

name: 48940d92-ff05-449e-9111-e742d9280451
display_name: Reserved System Pool Taints
description: Restricts the CriticalAddonsOnly taint to just the system pool. AKS uses the CriticalAddonsOnly taint to keep customer pods away from the system pool. It ensures a clear separation between AKS components and customer pods, as well as prevents customer pods from being evicted if they do not tolerate the CriticalAddonsOnly taint.

name: 49a22571-d204-4c91-a7b6-09b1a586fbc9
display_name: Keys should have the specified maximum validity period
description: Manage your organizational compliance requirements by specifying the maximum amount of time in days that a key can be valid within your key vault.

name: 4ee3ee6a-96ea-4d25-9c00-17f11d2e02c8
display_name: [Preview]: Sets Privilege escalation in the Pod spec in init containers to false.
description: Setting Privilege escalation to false in init containers increases security by preventing containers from allowing privilege escalation such as via set-user-ID or set-group-ID file mode.

name: 4f3823b6-6dac-4b5a-9c61-ce1afb829f17
display_name: Kubernetes clusters should use Container Storage Interface(CSI) driver StorageClass
description: The Container Storage Interface (CSI) is a standard for exposing arbitrary block and file storage systems to containerized workloads on Kubernetes. In-tree provisioner StorageClass should be deprecated since AKS version 1.21. To learn more, https://aka.ms/aks-csi-driver

name: 50c83470-d2f0-4dda-a716-1938a4825f62
display_name: Kubernetes cluster containers should only use allowed pull policy
description: Restrict containers' pull policy to enforce containers to use only allowed images on deployments

name: 511f5417-5d12-434d-ab2e-816901e72a5e
display_name: Kubernetes cluster containers should only use allowed AppArmor profiles
description: Containers should only use allowed AppArmor profiles in a Kubernetes cluster. This policy is generally available for Kubernetes Service (AKS), and preview for Azure Arc enabled Kubernetes. For more information, see https://aka.ms/kubepolicydoc.

name: 51fb1409-3c36-43c4-a750-ce814ac35ea6
display_name: Keys using AES cryptography should have a specified minimum key size
description: Set the minimum allowed key size for use with your key vaults. Use of AES keys with small key sizes is not a secure practice and doesn't meet many industry certification requirements.

name: 53a4a537-990c-495a-92e0-7c21a465442c
display_name: Cannot Edit Individual Nodes
description: Cannot Edit Individual Nodes. Users should not edit individual nodes. Please edit node pools. Modifying individual nodes can lead to inconsistent settings, operational challenges, and potential security risks.

name: 53c70b02-63dd-11ea-bc55-0242ac130003
display_name: [Preview]: Configure allowed module authors for specified Azure Machine Learning computes
description: Provide allowed module authors in specified Azure Machine Learning computes and can be assigned at the workspace. For more information, visit https://aka.ms/amlpolicydoc.

name: 5485eac0-7e8f-4964-998b-a44f4f0c1e75
display_name: Kubernetes cluster Windows containers should not run as ContainerAdministrator
description: Prevent usage of ContainerAdministrator as the user to execute the container processes for Windows pods or containers. This recommendation is intended to improve the security of Windows nodes. For more information, see https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/windows/intro/ .

name: 56d0a13f-712f-466b-8416-56fb354fb823
display_name: Kubernetes cluster containers should not use forbidden sysctl interfaces
description: Containers should not use forbidden sysctl interfaces in a Kubernetes cluster. This policy is generally available for Kubernetes Service (AKS), and preview for Azure Arc enabled Kubernetes. For more information, see https://aka.ms/kubepolicydoc.

name: 57dde185-5c62-4063-b965-afbb201e9c1c
display_name: Kubernetes cluster Windows containers should only run with approved user and domain user group
description: Control the user that Windows pods and containers can use to run in a Kubernetes Cluster. This recommendation is part of Pod Security Policies on Windows nodes which are intended to improve the security of your Kubernetes environments.

name: 57f274ef-580a-4ed2-bcf8-5c6fa3775253
display_name: Sets automountServiceAccountToken in the Pod spec in containers to false.
description: Setting automountServiceAccountToken to false increases security by avoiding the default auto-mounting of service account tokens

name: 5853517a-63de-11ea-bc55-0242ac130003
display_name: [Preview]: Configure allowed registries for specified Azure Machine Learning computes
description: Provide registries that are allowed in specified Azure Machine Learning computes and can be assigned at the workspace. For more information, visit https://aka.ms/amlpolicydoc.

name: 587c79fe-dd04-4a5e-9d0b-f89598c7261b
display_name: Keys should be backed by a hardware security module (HSM)
description: An HSM is a hardware security module that stores keys. An HSM provides a physical layer of protection for cryptographic keys. The cryptographic key cannot leave a physical HSM which provides a greater level of security than a software key.

name: 595f98a5-b562-4ab6-b9c8-2ff9f39a3f7c
display_name: [Preview]: Cognitive Services Agents should only use allowed control
description: Mandate minimum levels of multi-severity content filtering for harmful content for agents within your organization.

name: 5c58d54b-87f0-4dcd-83ea-e855fc988997
display_name: [Preview]: Sets UnhealthyPodEvictionPolicy to 'AlwaysAllow'
description: Sets Pod Disruption Budget's UnhealthyPodEvictionPolicy to 'AlwaysAllow' to allow for evicting even unhealthy pods when performing cluster administration

name: 5e5a0673-649e-4d50-bf9d-5a387a4e2081
display_name: [Preview]: Kubernetes cluster containers should use only allowed sysctl interfaces
description: Containers should use only allowed sysctl interfaces in a Kubernetes cluster. This policy is generally available for Kubernetes Service (AKS), and preview for Azure Arc enabled Kubernetes. For more information, see https://aka.ms/kubepolicydoc.

name: 5f86cb6e-c4da-441b-807c-44bd0cc14e66
display_name: [Deprecated]: Ensure only allowed container images in AKS
description: This policy ensures only allowed container images are running in an Azure Kubernetes Service cluster. This policy is deprecated, please visit https://aka.ms/kubepolicydoc for instructions on using new Kubernetes policies.

name: 5f86d473-38a8-46c9-bdfe-d7fa3b9836bf
display_name: Sets Kubernetes cluster containers memory limits to default values in case not present.
description: Setting container memory limits to prevent resource exhaustion attacks in a Kubernetes cluster.

name: 5ff38825-c5d8-47c5-b70e-069a21955146
display_name: Keys should have more than the specified number of days before expiration
description: If a key is too close to expiration, an organizational delay to rotate the key may result in an outage. Keys should be rotated at a specified number of days prior to expiration to provide sufficient time to react to a failure.

name: 65280eef-c8b4-425e-9aec-af55e55bf581
display_name: Kubernetes cluster should not use naked pods
description: Block usage of naked Pods. Naked Pods will not be rescheduled in the event of a node failure. Pods should be managed by Deployment, Replicset, Daemonset or Jobs

name: 6a6f7384-63de-11ea-bc55-0242ac130003
display_name: [Preview]: Configure code signing for training code for specified Azure Machine Learning computes
description: Provide code signing for training code in specified Azure Machine Learning computes and can be assigned at the workspace. For more information, visit https://aka.ms/amlpolicydoc.

name: 6bcd4321-fb89-4e3e-bf6c-999c13d47f43
display_name: [Preview]: Sets Kubernetes cluster init containers' secure computing mode profile type to RuntimeDefault if not present.
description: Setting secure computing mode profile type for init containers to prevent unauthorized and potentially harmful system calls to the kernel from user space.

name: 6f87d474-38a9-46c9-bdfe-d7fa3b9836bf
display_name: [Preview]: Sets Kubernetes cluster containers' secure computing mode profile type to RuntimeDefault if not present.
description: Setting secure computing mode profile type for containers to prevent unauthorized and potentially harmful system calls to the kernel from user space.

name: 75262d3e-ba4a-4f43-85f8-9f72c090e5e3
display_name: Secrets should have content type set
description: A content type tag helps identify whether a secret is a password, connection string, etc. Different secrets have different rotation requirements. Content type tag should be set on secrets.

name: 75c4f823-d65c-4f29-a733-01d0077fdbcb
display_name: Keys should be the specified cryptographic type RSA or EC
description: Some applications require the use of keys backed by a specific cryptographic type. Enforce a particular cryptographic key type, RSA or EC, in your environment.

name: 77eeea86-7e81-4a7d-9067-de844d096752
display_name: [Preview]: Configure allowed Python packages for specified Azure Machine Learning computes
description: Provide allowed Python packages in specified Azure Machine Learning computes and can be assigned at the workspace. For more information, visit https://aka.ms/amlpolicydoc.

name: 7ce7ac02-a5c6-45d6-8d1b-844feb1c1531
display_name: [Deprecated]: Do not allow privileged containers in AKS
description: This policy does not allow privileged containers creation in an Azure Kubernetes Service cluster. This policy is deprecated, please visit https://aka.ms/kubepolicydoc for instructions on using new Kubernetes policies.

name: 82067dbb-e53b-4e06-b631-546d197452d9
display_name: Keys using RSA cryptography should have a specified minimum key size
description: Set the minimum allowed key size for use with your key vaults. Use of RSA keys with small key sizes is not a secure practice and doesn't meet many industry certification requirements.

name: 82985f06-dc18-4a48-bc1c-b9f4f0098cfe
display_name: Kubernetes cluster pods should only use approved host network and port list
description: Restrict pod access to the host network and the allowable host ports in a Kubernetes cluster. This recommendation is part of CIS 5.2.4 which is intended to improve the security of your Kubernetes environments and aligns with Pod Security Standards (PSS) for hostPorts. This policy is generally available for Kubernetes Service (AKS), and preview for Azure Arc enabled Kubernetes. For more information, see https://aka.ms/kubepolicydoc.

name: 83a0809a-a4e3-4ef2-8a24-2afc156607af
display_name: [Deprecated]: No AKS Specific Labels. Versioning Test BuiltIn.
description: This is a test policy only for internal use by Policy team. Prevents customers from applying AKS specific labels

name: 85793e88-5a58-4555-93fa-4df63c86ae9c
display_name: [Deprecated]: Azure Machine Learning Model Registry Deployments are restricted except for the allowed Registry. Versioning Test BuiltIn.
description: Only deploy Registry Models in the allowed Registry and that are not restricted.

name: 86810a98-8e91-4a44-8386-ec66d0de5d57
display_name: [Preview]: Azure Key Vault Managed HSM keys using RSA cryptography should have a specified minimum key size
description: To use this policy in preview, you must first follow these instructions at https://aka.ms/mhsmgovernance. Set the minimum allowed key size for use with your key vaults. Use of RSA keys with small key sizes is not a secure practice and doesn't meet many industry certification requirements.

name: 8791d062-ba96-4c34-b604-8538f7e30ca0
display_name: [Preview]: Foundry model deployments should meet eligibility requirements
description: Ensures that model deployments in Microsoft Foundry comply with organizational eligibility requirements based on model attributes, such as availability status (preview or GA) and distribution source

name: 8e826246-c976-48f6-b03e-619bb92b3d82
display_name: Certificates should be issued by the specified integrated certificate authority
description: Manage your organizational compliance requirements by specifying the Azure integrated certificate authorities that can issue certificates in your key vault such as Digicert or GlobalSign.

name: 8e875f96-2c56-40ca-86db-b9f6a0be7347
display_name: Sets readOnlyRootFileSystem in the Pod spec to true if it is not set.
description: Setting readOnlyRootFileSystem to true increases security by preventing containers from writing into the root filesystem

name: 9224c1cc-34fc-44f1-ad08-5c4b079c9ce6
display_name: [Preview]: Cognitive Services Agents should only use allowed prompt content filtering
description: Mandate minimum levels of content filtering for prompt content for agents within your organization.

name: 930f48f9-f07e-427c-9494-52603581c6a9
display_name: [Preview]: Cognitive Services Deployments should only use allowed control
description: Mandate minimum levels of multi-severity content filtering for harmful content for model deployments within your organization.

name: 95edb821-ddaf-4404-9732-666045e056b4
display_name: Kubernetes cluster should not allow privileged containers
description: Do not allow privileged containers creation in a Kubernetes cluster. This recommendation is part of CIS 5.2.1 which is intended to improve the security of your Kubernetes environments. This policy is generally available for Kubernetes Service (AKS), and preview for Azure Arc enabled Kubernetes. For more information, see https://aka.ms/kubepolicydoc.

name: 975ce327-682c-4f2e-aa46-b9598289b86c
display_name: Kubernetes cluster containers should only use allowed seccomp profiles
description: Pod containers can only use allowed seccomp profiles in a Kubernetes cluster. This policy is generally available for Kubernetes Service (AKS), and preview for Azure Arc enabled Kubernetes. For more information, see https://aka.ms/kubepolicydoc.

name: 97de439f-fd35-4d43-a693-3644f51a51fd
display_name: [Preview]: Sets Kubernetes cluster init containers securityContext.runAsUser fields to 1000, a non-root user id
description: Reduces attack surface introduced by escalating privileges as root user in the presence of security vulnerabilities.

name: 98728c90-32c7-4049-8429-847dc0f4fe37
display_name: Key Vault secrets should have an expiration date
description: Secrets should have a defined expiration date and not be permanent. Secrets that are valid forever provide a potential attacker with more time to compromise them. It is a recommended security practice to set expiration dates on secrets.

name: 98cec160-6f57-4d11-86e2-0a03290a3a8a
display_name: [Deprecated]: Key Vault Managed HSM keys using elliptic curve cryptography should have the specified curve names. Versioning Test BuiltIn.
description: This is a test policy only for internal use by Policy team. Keys backed by elliptic curve cryptography can have different curve names. Some applications are only compatible with specific elliptic curve keys. Enforce the types of elliptic curve keys that are allowed to be created in your environment.

name: 9a5f4e39-e427-4d5d-ae73-93db00328bec
display_name: Kubernetes resources should have required annotations
description: Ensure that required annotations are attached on a given Kubernetes resource kind for improved resource management of your Kubernetes resources. This policy is generally available for Kubernetes Service (AKS), and preview for Azure Arc enabled Kubernetes. For more information, see https://aka.ms/kubepolicydoc.

name: 9f061a12-e40d-4183-a00e-171812443373
display_name: Kubernetes clusters should not use the default namespace
description: Prevent usage of the default namespace in Kubernetes clusters to protect against unauthorized access for ConfigMap, Pod, Secret, Service, and ServiceAccount resource types. For more information, see https://aka.ms/kubepolicydoc.

name: a22123bd-b9da-4c86-9424-24903e91fd55
display_name: No AKS Specific Labels
description: Prevents customers from applying AKS specific labels. AKS uses labels prefixed with `kubernetes.azure.com` to denote AKS owned components. The customer should not use these labels.

name: a22f4a40-01d3-4c7d-8071-da157eeff341
display_name: Certificates should be issued by the specified non-integrated certificate authority
description: Manage your organizational compliance requirements by specifying one custom or internal certificate authorities that can issue certificates in your key vault.

name: a27c700f-8a22-44ec-961c-41625264370b
display_name: Kubernetes clusters should not use specific security capabilities
description: Prevent specific security capabilities in Kubernetes clusters to prevent ungranted privileges on the Pod resource. For more information, see https://aka.ms/kubepolicydoc.

name: a2abc456-f0ae-464b-bd3a-07a3cdbd7fb1
display_name: Kubernetes cluster Windows containers should not overcommit cpu and memory
description: Windows container resource requests should be less or equal to the resource limit or unspecified to avoid overcommit. If Windows memory is over-provisioned it will process pages in disk - which can slow down performance - instead of terminating the container with out-of-memory

name: a2d3ed81-8d11-4079-80a5-1faadc0024f4
display_name: [Deprecated]: Ensure CPU and memory resource limits defined on containers in AKS
description: This policy ensures CPU and memory resource limits are defined on containers in an Azure Kubernetes Service cluster. This policy is deprecated, please visit https://aka.ms/kubepolicydoc for instructions on using new Kubernetes policies.

name: a3dc4946-dba6-43e6-950d-f96532848c9f
display_name: Kubernetes clusters should ensure that the cluster-admin role is only used where required
description: The role 'cluster-admin' provides wide-ranging powers over the environment and should be used only where and when needed.

name: a74d8f00-2fd9-4ce4-968e-0ee1eb821698
display_name: [Deprecated]: Enforce internal load balancers in AKS
description: This policy enforces load balancers do not have public IPs in an Azure Kubernetes Service cluster. This policy is deprecated, please visit https://aka.ms/kubepolicydoc for instructions on using new Kubernetes policies.

name: a8e3ce3c-cac3-4402-a28a-03ee3ede9790
display_name: [Preview]: Sets Kubernetes cluster container securityContext.runAsUser fields to 1000, a non-root user id
description: Reduces attack surface introduced by escalating privileges as root user in the presence of security vulnerabilities.

name: aafe3651-cb78-4f68-9f81-e7e41509110f
display_name: Foundry model deployments should only use approved models
description: Ensures that model deployments in Microsoft Foundry only use models explicitly approved by the organization, based on configured model identifiers or publishers.

name: ad27588c-0198-4c84-81ef-08efd0274653
display_name: [Preview]: Azure Key Vault Managed HSM Keys should have more than the specified number of days before expiration
description: To use this policy in preview, you must first follow these instructions at https://aka.ms/mhsmgovernance. If a key is too close to expiration, an organizational delay to rotate the key may result in an outage. Keys should be rotated at a specified number of days prior to expiration to provide sufficient time to react to a failure.

name: af253d37-136a-42f8-a1fc-30010c083d41
display_name: [Preview]: Cognitive Services Deployments should only use allowed completion content filtering
description: Mandate minimum levels of content filtering for completion content for model deployments within your organization.

name: b0eb591a-5e70-4534-a8bf-04b9c489584a
display_name: Secrets should have more than the specified number of days before expiration
description: If a secret is too close to expiration, an organizational delay to rotate the secret may result in an outage. Secrets should be rotated at a specified number of days prior to expiration to provide sufficient time to react to a failure.

name: b0fdedee-7b9e-4a17-9f5d-5e8e912d2f01
display_name: Kubernetes cluster services should use unique selectors
description: Ensure Services in a Namespace Have Unique Selectors. A unique service selector ensures that each service within a namespace is uniquely identifiable based on specific criteria. This policy syncs service resources into OPA via Gatekeeper. Before applying, verify Gatekeeper pods memory capacity won't be exceeded. Parameters apply to specific namespaces, but it syncs all resources of that type across all namespaces. Currently in preview for Kubernetes Service (AKS).

name: b1a9997f-2883-4f12-bdff-2280f99b5915
display_name: Ensure cluster containers have readiness or liveness probes configured
description: This policy enforces that all pods have a readiness and/or liveness probes configured. Probe Types can be any of tcpSocket, httpGet and exec. This policy is generally available for Kubernetes Service (AKS), and preview for Azure Arc enabled Kubernetes. For instructions on using this policy, visit https://aka.ms/kubepolicydoc.

name: b2fd3e59-6390-4f2b-8247-ea676bd03e2d
display_name: [Deprecated]: Enforce unique ingress hostnames across namespaces in Kubernetes cluster
description: This policy enforces unique ingress hostnames across namespaces in a Kubernetes cluster. For instructions on using this policy, please go to https://aka.ms/kubepolicydoc.

name: b81f454c-eebb-4e4f-9dfe-dca060e8a8fd
display_name: [Preview]: Kubernetes clusters should restrict creation of given resource type
description: Given Kubernetes resource type should not be deployed in certain namespace.

name: bd78111f-4953-4367-9fd5-7e08808b54bf
display_name: Certificates using elliptic curve cryptography should have allowed curve names
description: Manage the allowed elliptic curve names for ECC Certificates stored in key vault. More information can be found at https://aka.ms/akvpolicy.

name: c1ad46c6-37f8-4af0-9c71-c208375c87dd
display_name: [Preview]: Cognitive Services Deployments Should Only Use Allowed Control Mode
description: Mandate content filtering mode for model deployments within your organization.

name: c26596ff-4d70-4e6a-9a30-c2506bd2f80c
display_name: Kubernetes cluster containers should only use allowed capabilities
description: Restrict the capabilities to reduce the attack surface of containers in a Kubernetes cluster. This recommendation is part of CIS 5.2.8 and CIS 5.2.9 which are intended to improve the security of your Kubernetes environments. This policy is generally available for Kubernetes Service (AKS), and preview for Azure Arc enabled Kubernetes. For more information, see https://aka.ms/kubepolicydoc.

name: c26e4b24-cf98-4c67-b48b-5a25c4c69eb9
display_name: Keys should not be active for longer than the specified number of days
description: Specify the number of days that a key should be active. Keys that are used for an extended period of time increase the probability that an attacker could compromise the key. As a good security practice, make sure that your keys have not been active longer than two years.

name: c812272d-7488-495f-a505-047d34b83f58
display_name: Mutate K8s Init Container to drop all capabilities
description: Mutates securityContext.capabilities.drop to add in "ALL". This drops all capabilities for k8s linux init containers

name: c873b3ba-c605-42e4-a64b-a142a93826fc
display_name: Mutate K8s Container to drop all capabilities
description: Mutates securityContext.capabilities.drop to add in "ALL". This drops all capabilities for k8s linux containers

name: ca8d5704-aa2b-40cf-b110-dc19052825ad
display_name: Kubernetes clusters should minimize wildcard use in role and cluster role
description: Using wildcards '*' can be a security risk because it grants broad permissions that may not be necessary for a specific role. If a role has too many permissions, it could potentially be abused by an attacker or compromised user to gain unauthorized access to resources in the cluster.

name: cee51871-e572-4576-855c-047c820360f0
display_name: Certificates using RSA cryptography should have the specified minimum key size
description: Manage your organizational compliance requirements by specifying a minimum key size for RSA certificates stored in your key vault.

name: cf426bb8-b320-4321-8545-1b784a5df3a4
display_name: [Image Integrity] Kubernetes clusters should only use images signed by notation
description: Use images signed by notation to ensure that images come from trusted sources and will not be maliciously modified. For more info, visit https://aka.ms/aks/image-integrity

name: d011d9f7-ba32-4005-b727-b3d09371ca60
display_name: [Deprecated]: Enforce unique ingress hostnames across namespaces in AKS
description: This policy enforces unique ingress hostnames across namespaces in an Azure Kubernetes Service cluster. This policy is deprecated, please visit https://aka.ms/kubepolicydoc for instructions on using new Kubernetes policies.

name: d2e7ea85-6b44-4317-a0be-1b951587f626
display_name: Kubernetes clusters should not grant CAP_SYS_ADMIN security capabilities
description: To reduce the attack surface of your containers, restrict CAP_SYS_ADMIN Linux capabilities. For more information, see https://aka.ms/kubepolicydoc.

name: d3e82b87-6673-410b-8501-1896b688b9a3
display_name: Certificates should be issued by one of the specified non-integrated certificate authorities
description: Manage your organizational compliance requirements by specifying custom or internal certificate authorities that can issue certificates in your key vault.

name: d46c275d-1680-448d-b2ec-e495a3b6cc89
display_name: Kubernetes cluster services should only use allowed external IPs
description: Use allowed external IPs to avoid the potential attack (CVE-2020-8554) in a Kubernetes cluster. For more information, see https://aka.ms/kubepolicydoc.

name: d77df159-718b-4aca-b94b-8e8890a98231
display_name: [Preview]: Sets Privilege escalation in the Pod spec to false.
description: Setting Privilege escalation to false increases security by preventing containers from allowing privilege escalation such as via set-user-ID or set-group-ID file mode.

name: d77f191e-2338-45d0-b6d4-4ee1c586a192
display_name: Sets maxUnavailable pods to 1 for PodDisruptionBudget resources
description: Setting your max unavailable pod value to 1 ensures that your application or service is available during a disruption

name: d855fd7a-9be5-4d84-8b75-28d41aadc158
display_name: [Preview]: Load tests using Azure Load Testing should be run only against private endpoints from within a virtual network.
description: Azure Load Testing engine instances should use virtual network injection for the following purposes: 1. Isolate Azure Load Testing engines to a virtual network. 2. Enable Azure Load Testing engines to interact with systems in either on premises data centers or Azure service in other virtual networks. 3. Empower customers to control inbound and outbound network communications for Azure Load Testing engines.

name: d8c942c6-16a3-400b-8f2e-785f44030036
display_name: Kubernetes clusters should specify host in ingress resource rules
description: Ensure specifying host in ingress resource rules to prevent unintentional exposure of backend services to unauthorized access. This policy evaluates Kubernetes Ingress resources to ensure that each rule has a specified host field.

name: d8cf8476-a2ec-4916-896e-992351803c44
display_name: Keys should have a rotation policy ensuring that their rotation is scheduled within the specified number of days after creation.
description: Manage your organizational compliance requirements by specifying the maximum number of days after key creation until it must be rotated.

name: d9e8f2c1-4c5a-4f5c-8b5a-2abf1e9f7b4d
display_name: [Preview]: Kubernetes cluster should implement accurate Pod Disruption Budgets
description: Prevents faulty Pod Disruption Budgets, ensuring a minimum number of operational pods. Refer to the official Kubernetes documentation for details. Relies on Gatekeeper data replication and syncs all Deployment, StatefulSet, and PodDisruptionBudget resources scoped to it into OPA. Before applying this policy, ensure that the synced resources won't strain your memory capacity. All resources of these kinds across namespaces will sync. Note: currently in preview for Kubernetes Service (AKS).

name: df49d893-a74c-421d-bc95-c663042e5b80
display_name: Kubernetes cluster containers should run with a read only root file system
description: Run containers with a read only root file system to protect from changes at run-time with malicious binaries being added to PATH in a Kubernetes cluster. This policy is generally available for Kubernetes Service (AKS), and preview for Azure Arc enabled Kubernetes. For more information, see https://aka.ms/kubepolicydoc.

name: e16d171b-bfe5-4d79-a525-19736b396e92
display_name: Restricts the CriticalAddonsOnly taint to just the system pool.
description: To avoid eviction of user apps from user pools and maintain separation of concerns between the user and system pools, the 'CriticalAddonsOnly' taint should not be applied to user pools.

name: e1e6c427-07d9-46ab-9689-bfa85431e636
display_name: Kubernetes cluster pods and containers should follow SELinux security standards
description: This policy enforces Kubernetes Pod Security Standards for SELinux options. Under PSS mode, 'user' and 'role' fields must be empty, and 'type' field must be one of the allowed values. For more information, see https://aka.ms/kubepolicydoc.

name: e24df237-32cb-4a6c-a2f6-85b499cda9f2
display_name: Prints a message if a mutation is applied
description: Looks up the mutation annotations applied and prints a message if annotation exists.

name: e345eecc-fa47-480f-9e88-67dcc122b164
display_name: Kubernetes cluster containers CPU and memory resource limits should not exceed the specified limits
description: Enforce container CPU and memory resource limits to prevent resource exhaustion attacks in a Kubernetes cluster. This policy is generally available for Kubernetes Service (AKS), and preview for Azure Arc enabled Kubernetes. For more information, see https://aka.ms/kubepolicydoc.

name: e58fd0c1-feac-4d12-92db-0a7e9421f53e
display_name: [Preview]: Azure Key Vault Managed HSM keys using elliptic curve cryptography should have the specified curve names
description: To use this policy in preview, you must first follow these instructions at https://aka.ms/mhsmgovernance. Keys backed by elliptic curve cryptography can have different curve names. Some applications are only compatible with specific elliptic curve keys. Enforce the types of elliptic curve keys that are allowed to be created in your environment.

name: e8d99835-8a06-45ae-a8e0-87a91941ccfe
display_name: Secrets should not be active for longer than the specified number of days
description: If your secrets were created with an activation date set in the future, you must ensure that your secrets have not been active for longer than the specified duration.

name: f06ddb64-5fa3-4b77-b166-acb36f7f6042
display_name: Kubernetes cluster pods and containers should only run with approved user and group IDs
description: Control the user, primary group, supplemental group and file system group IDs that pods and containers can use to run in a Kubernetes Cluster. This policy is generally available for Kubernetes Service (AKS), and preview for Azure Arc enabled Kubernetes. For more information, see https://aka.ms/kubepolicydoc.

name: f3a9c2e0-7b4d-4d8f-9c3a-2e1f6b9a8d4e
display_name: [Preview]: Cognitive Services Deployments should only use allowed prompt content filtering
description: Mandate minimum levels of content filtering for prompt content for model deployments within your organization.

name: f4a8fce0-2dd5-4c21-9a36-8f0ec809d663
display_name: Kubernetes cluster pod FlexVolume volumes should only use allowed drivers
description: Pod FlexVolume volumes should only use allowed drivers in a Kubernetes cluster. This policy is generally available for Kubernetes Service (AKS), and preview for Azure Arc enabled Kubernetes. For more information, see https://aka.ms/kubepolicydoc.

name: f772fb64-8e40-40ad-87bc-7706e1949427
display_name: Certificates should not expire within the specified number of days
description: Manage certificates that will expire within a specified number of days to ensure your organization has sufficient time to rotate the certificate prior to expiration.

name: f85eb0dd-92ee-40e9-8a76-db25a507d6d3
display_name: Kubernetes cluster containers should only use allowed ProcMountType
description: Pod containers can only use allowed ProcMountTypes in a Kubernetes cluster. This policy is generally available for Kubernetes Service (AKS), and preview for Azure Arc enabled Kubernetes. For more information, see https://aka.ms/kubepolicydoc.

name: f8d398ae-0441-4921-a341-40f3973d4647
display_name: [Deprecated]: Azure Data Factory pipelines should only communicate with allowed domains. Versioning Test BuiltIn
description: This is a test policy only for internal use by Policy team. To prevent data & token exfiltration, set the domains that Azure Data Factory should be allowed to communicate with. Note: While in public preview, the compliance for this policy is not reported, & for policy to be applied to Data Factory, please enable outbound rules functionality in the ADF studio. For more information, visit https://aka.ms/data-exfiltration-policy.

name: fa85a975-a142-46dc-a9ab-8cc69ecb358c
display_name: Azure Key Vault Managed HSM keys using AES cryptography should have a specified minimum key size
description: To use this policy, you must first follow these instructions at https://aka.ms/mhsmgovernance. Set the minimum allowed key size for use with your Managed HSM. Use of AES keys with small key sizes is not a secure practice and doesn't meet many industry certification requirements.

name: fa8af49a-f61d-4f56-9138-46b77d37df43
display_name: [Deprecated]: Keys should have a rotation policy within the specified number of days after creation. Versioning Test BuiltIn.
description: This is a test policy only for internal use by Policy team. Manage your organizational compliance requirements by specifying the maximum number of days after key creation until it must be rotated.

name: fe74a23d-79e4-401c-bd0d-fd7a5b35af32
display_name: [Preview]: Sets Kubernetes cluster Pod securityContext.runAsUser fields to 1000, a non-root user id
description: Reduces attack surface introduced by escalating privileges as root user in the presence of security vulnerabilities.

name: febd0533-8e55-448f-b837-bd0e06f16469
display_name: Kubernetes cluster containers should only use allowed images
description: Use images from trusted registries to reduce the Kubernetes cluster's exposure risk to unknown vulnerabilities, security issues and malicious images. For more information, see https://aka.ms/kubepolicydoc.

name: fed6510d-00b9-40db-a347-933125a6a327
display_name: [Preview]: Prevents init containers from being ran as root by setting runAsNotRoot to true.
description: Setting runAsNotRoot to true increases security by preventing containers from being ran as root.

name: ff25f3c8-b739-4538-9d07-3d6d25cfb255
display_name: Keys using elliptic curve cryptography should have the specified curve names
description: Keys backed by elliptic curve cryptography can have different curve names. Some applications are only compatible with specific elliptic curve keys. Enforce the types of elliptic curve keys that are allowed to be created in your environment.
